title: SUB GEOGRAPHY TOPIC 1 PLAINS AND ISLANDS 2 HIMALAYAN R

questions.question: 1. Which river systems formed the Northern Plains of India? / भारत के विशाल मैदानों का निर्माण किन नदी प्रणालियों द्वारा किया गया है?
options: Ganga, Yamuna, Narmada / गंगा, यमुना, नर्मदा
Krishna, Kaveri, Godavari / कृष्णा, कावेरी, गोदावरी
Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra / सिंधु, गंगा, ब्रह्मपुत्र
Indus, Tapti, Luni / सिंधु, ताप्ती, लूणी
questions.explanation: The Northern Plains are formed by the interplay of the Indus, Ganga, and Brahmaputra rivers.

questions.question: 2. Which region lies south of the Shiwaliks and is known for pebble deposition? / शिवालिक के दक्षिण में स्थित कौन सा क्षेत्र कंकड़-पत्थरों के निक्षेपण के लिए जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Bhabar is a narrow belt where rivers disappear underground due to porous pebble-laden soil.

questions.question: 3. In which region do underground streams reappear to create a marshy area? / किस क्षेत्र में भूमिगत नदियाँ पुनः प्रकट होकर दलदली क्षेत्र बनाती हैं?
questions.explanation: The Terai is a wet, swampy, and marshy region located south of the Bhabar belt.

questions.question: 4. Which part of the plains represents the older alluvium? / मैदानों का कौन सा भाग पुरानी जलोढ़ मिट्टी का प्रतिनिधित्व करता है?
questions.explanation: Bhangar is the older alluvial soil that lies above the floodplains and often contains lime nodules.

questions.question: 5. What are the lime nodules found in Bhangar soil called? / भांगर मिट्टी में पाए जाने वाले चूने के निक्षेपों को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Bhangar region contains calcareous deposits locally known as Kankar.

questions.question: 6. Which part of the plains is most fertile and ideal for intensive agriculture? / मैदानों का कौन सा भाग सबसे उपजाऊ है और गहन कृषि के लिए आदर्श है?
questions.explanation: Khadar is the newer, younger alluvium deposited by floods every year, making it very fertile.

questions.question: 7. What is the area between two rivers called? / दो नदियों के बीच के क्षेत्र को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: 'Doab' is made up of two words: 'Do' (two) and 'Ab' (water/river).

questions.question: 8. Which state has the largest share of the Ganga Plains? / गंगा के मैदानों का सबसे बड़ा हिस्सा किस राज्य में है?
questions.explanation: The Ganga Plain spreads across North India, with Uttar Pradesh holding a significant portion.

questions.question: 9. The Brahmaputra Plain lies mainly in which state? / ब्रह्मपुत्र का मैदान मुख्य रूप से किस राज्य में स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra Plain forms the eastern part of the Northern Plains, primarily in Assam.

questions.question: 10. Which is the largest riverine island in the world? / विश्व का सबसे बड़ा नदी द्वीप कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Majuli, located in the Brahmaputra river in Assam, is the largest inhabited riverine island.

questions.question: 11. Where are the Andaman and Nicobar Islands located? / अंडमान और निकोबार द्वीप समूह कहाँ स्थित हैं?
options: Gulf of Kutch / कच्छ की खाड़ी
Bay of Bengal / बंगाल की खाड़ी
questions.explanation: These islands are located in the Bay of Bengal and are elevated portions of submarine mountains.

questions.question: 12. Which channel separates the Andaman Islands from the Nicobar Islands? / कौन सा चैनल अंडमान द्वीप समूह को निकोबार द्वीप समूह से अलग करता है?
questions.explanation: The 10° North latitude (Ten Degree Channel) separates the two island groups.

questions.question: 13. India's only active volcano is found on which island? / भारत का एकमात्र सक्रिय ज्वालामुखी किस द्वीप पर पाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Barren Island in the Andaman Sea is home to the only active volcano in South Asia.

questions.question: 14. What is the southernmost point of India located in the Nicobar Islands? / निकोबार द्वीप समूह में स्थित भारत का सबसे दक्षिणी बिंदु कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Indira Point, located on Great Nicobar Island, is the southernmost point of the Indian territory.

questions.question: 15. Lakshadweep islands are composed of which material? / लक्षद्वीप समूह किस सामग्री से बने हैं?
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep is a group of atolls and islands of coral origin in the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: 16. What was the earlier name of the Lakshadweep islands? / लक्षद्वीप समूह का पुराना नाम क्या था?
options: Laccadive, Minicoy and Amindivi / लक्कादीव, मिनिकॉय और अमीनदीवी
questions.explanation: They were renamed Lakshadweep in 1973.

questions.question: 17. Which is the largest island in the Lakshadweep group? / लक्षद्वीप समूह का सबसे बड़ा द्वीप कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Andrott (or Androth) is the largest island of the Lakshadweep archipelago.

questions.question: 18. Which island in Lakshadweep has a bird sanctuary and is uninhabited? / लक्षद्वीप का कौन सा द्वीप निर्जन है और वहां एक पक्षी अभयारण्य है?
questions.explanation: Pitti Island is a bird sanctuary where no humans reside.

questions.question: 19. Which channel separates the Lakshadweep group from the Maldives? / कौन सा चैनल लक्षद्वीप समूह को मालदीव से अलग करता है?
questions.explanation: The Eight Degree Channel separates the island of Minicoy from the Maldives.

questions.question: 20. Which channel separates Minicoy from the rest of Lakshadweep? / कौन सा चैनल मिनिकॉय को शेष लक्षद्वीप से अलग करता है?
questions.explanation: The 9 Degree Channel lies between Minicoy and the northern Lakshadweep islands.

questions.question: 21. Abdul Kalam Island (Wheeler Island) is located off the coast of which state? / अब्दुल कलाम द्वीप (व्हीलर द्वीप) किस राज्य के तट पर स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Located in Odisha, it is used by India as a primary missile testing facility.

questions.question: 22. Sriharikota island is famous for which of the following? / श्रीहरिकोटा द्वीप निम्नलिखित में से किसके लिए प्रसिद्ध है?
options: Nuclear power plant / परमाणु ऊर्जा संयंत्र
Space launch center / अंतरिक्ष प्रक्षेपण केंद्र
questions.explanation: It houses the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC), India's primary satellite launch site.

questions.question: 23. Which island is located between India and Sri Lanka? / भारत और श्रीलंका के बीच कौन सा द्वीप स्थित है?
options: New Moore Island / न्यू मूर द्वीप
questions.explanation: Pamban Island, also known as Rameswaram Island, is connected to mainland India by a bridge.

questions.question: 24. New Moore Island was a bone of contention between India and? / न्यू मूर द्वीप भारत और किसके बीच विवाद का विषय था?
questions.explanation: This uninhabited island in the Bay of Bengal emerged after a cyclone and has since largely disappeared.

questions.question: 25. Where is the Dr. Salim Ali Bird Sanctuary located? / डॉ. सलीम अली पक्षी अभयारण्य कहाँ स्थित है?
options: Majuli Island, Assam / माजुली द्वीप, असम
Havelock Island, Andaman / हैवलॉक द्वीप, अंडमान
Chorao Island, Goa / चोराव द्वीप, गोवा
questions.explanation: It is a famous sanctuary located on Chorao Island along the Mandovi River in Goa.

questions.question: 26. Which type of soil is predominantly found in the Northern Plains? / उत्तर के मैदानों में मुख्य रूप से किस प्रकार की मिट्टी पाई जाती है?
questions.explanation: The entire Northern Plain is made of alluvial soil deposited by Himalayan rivers.

questions.question: 27. What is the average width of the Northern Plains? / उत्तर के मैदानों की औसत चौड़ाई कितनी है?
questions.explanation: The plains have a width ranging between 150 km to 300 km.

questions.question: 28. Which is the administrative headquarters of Lakshadweep? / लक्षद्वीप का प्रशासनिक मुख्यालय कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Kavaratti is the capital and administrative center of the UT of Lakshadweep.

questions.question: 29. The "Saddle Peak" is the highest point of which island group? / "सैडल पीक" किस द्वीप समूह का उच्चतम बिंदु है?
options: Andaman and Nicobar / अंडमान और निकोबार
Daman and Diu / दमन और दीव
questions.explanation: Saddle Peak (732m) is the highest peak in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

questions.question: 30. Srirangam, a famous riverine island, is located in which river? / प्रसिद्ध नदी द्वीप श्रीरंगम किस नदी में स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Srirangam is a large riverine island in Tamil Nadu formed by the Kaveri River.

questions.question: 1. Which drainage pattern resembles the branching roots of a tree? / कौन सा अपवाह प्रतिरूप पेड़ की शाखाओं वाली जड़ों के समान दिखता है?
questions.explanation: Dendritic pattern is common in the rivers of Northern Plains like Ganga and Indus.

questions.question: 2. In which pattern do tributaries join the main river at right angles? / किस प्रतिरूप में सहायक नदियाँ मुख्य नदी से समकोण पर मिलती हैं?
questions.explanation: Trellis pattern forms where primary tributaries flow parallel and others join at 90 degrees.

questions.question: 3. Which pattern is formed when rivers radiate outward from a central peak? / जब नदियाँ एक केंद्रीय शिखर से बाहर की ओर निकलती हैं, तो कौन सा प्रतिरूप बनता है?
questions.explanation: Rivers like Narmada and Son originating from Amarkantak hills show a radial pattern.

questions.question: 4. Where does the Indus River originate? / सिंधु नदी का उद्गम कहाँ से होता है?
options: Bokhar Chu Glacier / बोखर चू ग्लेशियर
questions.explanation: The Indus originates near Mansarovar Lake in Tibet at an altitude of 4,164 m.

questions.question: 5. What is the Indus River called in Tibet? / तिब्बत में सिंधु नदी को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: In Tibet, Indus is known as 'Singi Khamban', which means 'Lion’s mouth'.

questions.question: 6. Which is the largest tributary of the Indus? / सिंधु की सबसे बड़ी सहायक नदी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Chenab is formed by two streams, Chandra and Bhaga, and is the largest Indus tributary.

questions.question: 7. Through which pass does the Satluj river enter India? / सतलज नदी किस दर्रे से भारत में प्रवेश करती है?
questions.explanation: Satluj enters India through Shipki La pass in Himachal Pradesh.

questions.question: 8. Where does the Ganga river officially take its name? / गंगा नदी आधिकारिक तौर पर अपना नाम कहाँ ग्रहण करती है?
questions.explanation: At Devprayag, Bhagirathi and Alaknanda meet to form the Ganga.

questions.question: 9. Which is the longest tributary of the Ganga? / गंगा की सबसे लंबी सहायक नदी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Yamuna originates from Yamunotri glacier and joins Ganga at Prayagraj.

questions.question: 10. Which river is known as the "Sorrow of Bihar"? / किस नदी को "बिहार का शोक" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Kosi is notorious for frequently changing its course and causing floods.

questions.question: 11. What is the Ganga called in Bangladesh? / बांग्लादेश में गंगा को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: After entering Bangladesh, the Ganga is known as the Padma.

questions.question: 12. The world's largest delta, Sundarbans, is formed by? / विश्व का सबसे बड़ा डेल्टा, सुंदरबन, किसके द्वारा बनाया गया है?
questions.explanation: It is formed by the confluence of the Padma (Ganga) and Jamuna (Brahmaputra) rivers.

questions.question: 13. Where does the Brahmaputra River originate? / ब्रह्मपुत्र नदी का उद्गम कहाँ से होता है?
questions.explanation: It originates in the Chemayungdung/Angsi glacier near Mansarovar lake.

questions.question: 14. What is the Brahmaputra called in Tibet? / तिब्बत में ब्रह्मपुत्र को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: In Tibet, it flows eastwards as the Yarlung Tsangpo.

questions.question: 15. Through which gorge does the Brahmaputra enter India? / ब्रह्मपुत्र किस गार्ज से भारत में प्रवेश करती है?
questions.explanation: It takes a sharp U-turn at Namcha Barwa and enters Arunachal Pradesh as Dihang.

questions.question: 16. Which is the world's largest riverine island? / विश्व का सबसे बड़ा नदी द्वीप कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Majuli island is located on the Brahmaputra river in Assam.

questions.question: 17. Which is the world's smallest riverine island? / विश्व का सबसे छोटा नदी द्वीप कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Umananda (Peacock Island) is also located on the Brahmaputra in Assam.

questions.question: 18. Which river is a right-bank tributary of the Brahmaputra? / कौन सी नदी ब्रह्मपुत्र की दाईं ओर की सहायक नदी है?
questions.explanation: Manas, Teesta, and Kameng are important right-bank tributaries.

questions.question: 19. The Bhupen Hazarika Bridge is built on which river? / भूपेन हजारिका सेतु किस नदी पर बना है?
questions.explanation: This bridge (Dhola-Sadiya) connects Assam and Arunachal over the Lohit river.

questions.question: 20. Which river joins the Alaknanda at Rudraprayag? / रुद्रप्रयाग में अलकनंदा से कौन सी नदी मिलती है?
questions.explanation: Alaknanda and Mandakini meet at Rudraprayag.

questions.question: 21. Where do Alaknanda and Pindar rivers meet? / अलकनंदा और पिंडर नदियाँ कहाँ मिलती हैं?
questions.explanation: The confluence of Alaknanda and Pindar is at Karnaprayag.

questions.question: 22. Which river is known as the "Sorrow of Bengal"? / किस नदी को "बंगाल का शोक" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Damodar was historically called the sorrow of Bengal due to devastating floods.

questions.question: 23. Lucknow is situated on the banks of which river? / लखनऊ किस नदी के तट पर स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Gomti is a tributary of the Ganga that flows through Lucknow.

questions.question: 24. Which is the only major Himalayan river that flows through a rift valley? / रिफ्ट घाटी से होकर बहने वाली एकमात्र प्रमुख हिमालयी नदी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Parts of the Indus and Damodar flow through structural troughs/rift valleys.

questions.question: 25. The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in which year? / सिंधु जल संधि पर किस वर्ष हस्ताक्षर किए गए थे?
questions.explanation: The treaty between India and Pakistan was brokered by the World Bank in 1960.

questions.question: 26. Which river is known as the 'Tsangpo' in Tibet? / तिब्बत में किस नदी को 'सांग्पो' कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Tsangpo means 'Purifier' in the Tibetan language.

questions.question: 27. Ayodhya is located on the banks of which river? / अयोध्या किस नदी के तट पर स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The city of Ayodhya in UP is situated along the Sarayu river.

questions.question: 28. Which tributary of the Ganga originates from the Amarkantak plateau? / गंगा की कौन सी सहायक नदी अमरकंटक पठार से निकलती है?
questions.explanation: Son is a major South-bank tributary of the Ganga originating in MP.

questions.question: 29. What is the Brahmaputra called in Bangladesh? / बांग्लादेश में ब्रह्मपुत्र को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: It enters Bangladesh as the Jamuna and later joins the Padma.

questions.question: 30. The combined flow of Ganga and Brahmaputra is finally known as? / गंगा और ब्रह्मपुत्र की संयुक्त धारा को अंततः किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: After joining the Barak/Meghna river, the combined stream is called Meghna.
